claim_policy.reason: This file maps Python EVAS behaviors to Rust primitive status. It is not same-slice EVAS/Spectre timing evidence.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_evaluate_static_affine
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Legacy narrow affine helper; not the target full expression IR.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_evaluate_static_linear
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Covers static-linear subsets only; dynamic/event/nonlinear cases fallback.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_evaluate_transition_targets
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Covers target/delay/rise/fall expression evaluation, not transition state evolution.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_evaluate_ordered_transition_segment
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Ordered shadow batch; Python still owns production semantics.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_next_transition_breakpoint
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Breakpoint scan helper only; transition state stepping is covered separately by evas_rust_transition_state_step.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_transition_state_step
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Typed-array transition state evolution primitive. 085 wires an opt-in engine production path through _transition(); it is still per-call production, not a whole-segment transition batch or speed claim.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_next_timer_breakpoint
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Breakpoint scan helper; production hook is opt-in and gated away for small timer sets because top-wall evidence showed length-1 FFI is not profitable.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_timer_periodic_step
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Typed-array periodic timer due/reschedule primitive; opt-in production hook exists behind a small-timer gate, while event body/lifecycle remain Python.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_timer_absolute_step
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Typed-array absolute timer due/expire primitive; opt-in production hook exists behind a small-timer gate after CPPLL showed per-check length-1 FFI slowdown.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_cross_detector_step
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Typed-array cross detector state update primitive; EVAS_RUST_EVENT_DUE_SHADOW validates detector parity. 085 adds opt-in Rust event-time interpolation cache for cross trigger/interpolation nodes, which event bodies can read through _get_voltage(); detector production ordering and event body execution remain Python.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_above_detector_step
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Typed-array above detector state update primitive; EVAS_RUST_EVENT_DUE_SHADOW validates detector parity. 085 adds opt-in Rust event-time interpolation cache for above trigger/interpolation nodes, which event bodies can read through _get_voltage(); production event dispatch remains Python.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_interpolate_event_values
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: 085 event-time voltage interpolation primitive. Opt-in engine path caches cross/above trigger/interpolation node values at detector crossing time; event body reads of those nodes can hit the cache, while detector ordering and event body execution remain Python-owned.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_dynamic_bus_offsets
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Base+offset node-id computation primitive for bounded 1-D/2-D dynamic bus accesses; compiler/runtime production path is not wired yet.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_event_lfsr_shift_xor_step
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Opt-in production primitive for one high-frequency event-body pattern: gated static-width LFSR shift/XOR with scalar state output. General event body/order/transition output execution remains Python-owned.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_event_static_linear_write
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: 081 compiler/runtime path for simple event-body state assignment write-sets. It reuses evas_rust_evaluate_static_linear rather than adding a new Rust ABI symbol; due/order remain Python-owned. 085 removes the conservative cross/above body node-read fallback for the opt-in interpolation-cache subset by auto-installing the interpolation backend when event-write Rust shadow/production is enabled.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_timer_static_linear_trace
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: 082 production whole-segment executor for one periodic timer with state-only static-linear event body, non-event static-linear output contributions, and explicit record_step trace plus timer breakpoints. 084 keeps this single-timer ABI for the one-timer case; multi-timer ordering is covered by evas_rust_timer_static_linear_queue_trace. 085 lets the engine feed a restricted default adaptive/internal time grid into this Rust trace when record_step is None. cross/above full event queue, transitions, dynamic arrays, and general node-reading event bodies remain outside this generic path.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_timer_static_linear_queue_trace
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: 084 production queue executor for multiple periodic timers with state-only static-linear event bodies, chronological fire-time ordering, source-order ties, non-event static-linear output contributions, and explicit record_step trace plus timer breakpoints. 085 lets the engine feed a restricted default adaptive/internal time grid into this Rust trace when record_step is None. cross/above full event queue, transition batch, dynamic arrays, node-reading event bodies, and absolute/state-owned timers remain outside this path.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_record_values_for_node_ids
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: 083 production record primitive for indexed node-id traces. It moves recorded-node value gather into Rust when indexed array loop is enabled; Python still owns time-point append, SimResult assembly, CSV emission, and checker contract.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_prbs7_trace
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Pre-069 whole-model production trace for semantic PRBS7/LFSR candidates. This is model-segment coverage, not a general implementation of every covered behavior id.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_gain_timer_reduction_trace
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: 069 production Rust whole-segment trace for gain_timer_reduction_v1 candidates. It owns source sampling, timer reduction, transition output evaluation, and trace matrix generation for this semantic family only.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_cmp_delay_trace
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: 070 production Rust whole-segment trace for cmp_delay_log_transition_v1 plus edge_interval_timer_v1 candidates. It owns clock edge detection, log-delay calculation, transition output evaluation, delay measurement, and trace matrix generation for this semantic family only.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_sar_loop_trace
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: 071 production Rust whole-segment trace for weighted_sar_adc_v1 plus weighted_dac_v1 plus sample_hold_rising_v1 candidates. It owns sample-hold update, clock-edge SAR event body, scalar/array state, transition output evaluation, and trace matrix generation for this semantic family only.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_gain_measurement_flow_trace
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: 075 production Rust whole-flow trace for vin_src plus lfsr plus dither_adder plus gain_amp_fixed measurement-flow candidates. It owns clocked vin target replay, LFSR dither state, dither addition, fixed gain amplification, transition output evaluation, and trace matrix generation for this semantic family only.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_copy_f64
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Utility helper, not a simulator behavior by itself.

implemented_rust_abi.name: evas_rust_max_err_ratio
implemented_rust_abi.scope_note: Array scan helper; full adaptive-step integration remains guarded by dirty/snapshot state.

behavior_catalog.name: timer_event_due_reschedule_and_breakpoint
behavior_catalog.scope_note: 060 adds a compiler-level Python due-mask batch for consecutive constant/parameter timer events. 061 adds a conservative Python fast path for scalar real state-owned absolute timers such as timer(t_next_toggle), skipping target-state reads before the armed time. 082-084 add production Rust whole-segment executors for periodic timer plus state-only static-linear event bodies, including multi-timer chronological/source-order queue execution. 085 removes the explicit-record-only restriction for this restricted timer-static-linear segment by feeding default adaptive/internal trace points into the Rust executor when record_step is None. General dynamic timer expressions, externally written timer target states, node-reading event bodies, event-owned output writes, and cross/above/transition queues remain on fallback paths.

behavior_catalog.name: adaptive_error_ratio_scan
behavior_catalog.scope_note: 085 default trace support is restricted to timer-static-linear whole-segment candidates and mirrors their internal/adaptive time grid before Rust execution. General adaptive err-ratio ownership remains partial.

behavior_catalog.name: per_step_model_lifecycle
behavior_catalog.rust_primitive: evas_rust_timer_static_linear_trace + evas_rust_timer_static_linear_queue_trace + model-specific whole-segment trace ABIs
